title: ChainAndTasksForged payload
description: OPTIONAL grouping/analytics signal for a chain-create-with-tasks — NOT a do-everything composite, despite the name. It does NOT create the chain or its tasks: the chain comes from the ChainCreated event and each task from its own TaskCreated event, and NO projection folds this event (it touches no entity state). It is a parent+children SUMMARY that sits ALONGSIDE those per-entity events (carrying task_count + the task_slugs + the entry `mode`), which the chain-close-completeness audit + analytics consumers join on. So: when creating a chain-with-tasks via `record`, the load-bearing events are ChainCreated + one TaskCreated per task (in that order); emit ChainAndTasksForged too ONLY if you want the grouping signal — the chain + tasks are complete without it. (forge(schema_name='chain', tasks=[…]) emits all of them for you.) The `mode` field discriminates the task-entry shape: 'full_objects' (every entry a full task object), 'pipe_delimited' (slug|scope|status strings), 'mixed', or 'empty' (chain forged with no tasks). T1 design audit added this as one of the eight original event types; landed in T7 of work-batching-and-forge-templates.

chain_slug.description: The chain that was created with these tasks. Matches the slug on the sibling ChainCreated event.

chain_id.description: Numeric chain id resolved at create time. Optional because the projection fold assigns the id; on a fresh chain emit the chain_id MAY be unavailable until the fold lands.

task_slugs.description: Per-task slugs in creation order. Empty when mode='empty'.

task_count.description: Number of tasks created with the chain. Matches len(task_slugs).

mode.description: Which shape the caller used for the tasks list. Analytics signal: T8's retro reports on how often agents use the full-object shape vs the legacy pipe-delimited shape.

per_task_rationales.description: Per-task rationale strings (parallel to task_slugs). Empty strings for pipe-delimited entries (which don't carry a rationale). Required-on-full-object validation runs at HandleForge entry; this field is informational on the events ledger.
